summary: Verified corrupted localStorage recovery patch in ChatContext.js. All 3 requested features tested and passing: (1) App recovers without crash from corrupted localStorage shape, (2) Chat tab row appears on Android viewport after load, (3) No runtime exceptions in ChatContext initialization. Fixed minor bug where pausedModels array validation passed due to typeof [] === 'object' in JavaScript.
critical_code_review_comments: normalizeSavedState() correctly sanitizes all malformed localStorage values to safe defaults
pausedModels: array -> empty object fallback (fixed !Array.isArray() check)
No React crashes or page errors with fully corrupted localStorage state
seed_data_creation: Created test user session via MongoDB for authenticated testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 13 tested corrupted localStorage recovery. normalizeSavedState() in ChatContext.js successfully sanitizes all malformed values (selectedModels as string, invalid activeTopTab, non-integer visibleModelIndex, non-array messages, array pausedModels, invalid contextMode/sharedRoomMode). Applied fix: Added !Array.isArray() check for pausedModels validation since typeof [] === 'object' in JavaScript. All tests pass on Android viewport (390x844).

corrupted_localstorage_tests: selectedModels as string -> normalized to DEFAULT_MODELS array - PASSED
activeTopTab as invalid string -> normalized to 'chat' - PASSED
visibleModelIndex as string -> normalized to 0 - PASSED
messages as string -> normalized to empty array - PASSED
pausedModels as array -> normalized to empty object - PASSED (after fix)
No page errors after loading /chat with corrupted state - PASSED
No runtime exceptions in ChatContext initialization - PASSED
android_viewport_tests: Chat tab row visible on Android - PASSED
App remains interactive after /chat load - PASSED
Side menu opens on click - PASSED

fix_applied.reason: typeof [] === 'object' in JavaScript, so arrays were incorrectly passing the object check
